q: Please don't neglect any details even though they seem to be unimportant. (Change the sentence, using 'no matter .......'.) 2024
a: Please don't neglect any details no matter how unimportant they seem to be.

q: These teenagers are very rude to the old man. But he is very nice to them. (Join the sentences, using 'no matter ...'.) 2024
a: No matter how rude to the old man these teenagers are, he is very nice to them.

q: The instructions may be confusing, but I will figure it out. (Use 'No matter how ...'.) 2024
a: No matter how confusing the instructions may be, I will figure it out.

q: The mathematical problems in this chapter are hard, but he can solve them very quickly. (Use 'no matter...'.) 2024
a: No matter how hard the mathematical problems in this chapter are, he can solve them very quickly.

q: Ko Myo tries hard to please his wife. She always finds something to complain about. (Join the sentences, using 'no matter...'.) 2024
a: No matter how hard Ko Myo tries to please his wife, she always finds something to complain about.

q: I don't care if it rains or freezes. I will go shopping tomorrow. (Join the sentences, using 'No matter...'.) 2024
a: No matter whether it rains or freezes, I will go shopping tomorrow.

q: He is found innocent, or he is found guilty. Nonetheless, his reputation is permanently damaged. (Join the sentences, beginning with 'No matter...'.) 2025
a: No matter whether he is found innocent or guilty, his reputation is permanently damaged.

q: Her workload is extremely demanding. She makes herself accessible to people, anyway. (Join the sentences, beginning with 'No matter'.) 2025
a: No matter how demanding her workload is, she makes herself accessible to people, anyway.

q: The problem seems to be difficult, but I'll find a solution. (Join the sentences, using 'No matter how...'.) 2025
a: No matter how difficult the problem seems to be, I'll find a solution.

q: It does not matter how often I warn them. They keep gambling. (Join, beginning with 'No matter...'.) 2025
a: No matter how often I warn them, they keep gambling.

q: She always listens patiently to her friends, even when their stories are repetitive. (Use 'No matter how...'.) 2025
a: She always listens patiently to her friends, no matter how repetitive their stories are.

q: She tries hard to please her boss. He is never satisfied. (Join the sentences, using 'No matter how....') 2025
a: No matter how hard she tries to please her boss, he is never satisfied.

q: Thuzar feels, very sorry for happened. The damage has been done and there is no way to reverse it (Join the sentences, using 'no matter ...') 2025
a: No matter how sorry Thuzar feels for what happened, the damage has been done and there is no way to reverse it.

q: She does not seem to lose any weight even though she tries very hard. (Join the sentences. beginning with 'No matter....') 2026
a: No matter how hard she tries, she does not seem to lose any weight.

q: My mother is very hard to please, but my brother always knows how to make her happy. (Use ' No matter...') 2026
a: No matter how hard my mother is to please, my brother always knows how to make her happy.
